diagnosis: In 'read-image-file', read_media_file's base64 jargon and lack of a clear 'view image' cue made the agent second-guess and fall back to read_file and get_file_info. In 'compare-two-configs', read_text_file's 'single file' framing invited per-file calls, while read_multiple_files' 'more efficient' wording made it sound optional rather than required for multi-file reads. In 'list-directory-flat', search_files' broad 'finding files' description let it look like a suitable way to list a directory, while list_directory didn't explicitly claim flat listing. In 'create-new-file', create_directory's 'ensure required paths exist' phrase tricked the agent into treating it as a prerequisite for write_file. In 'make-nested-directory', create_directory's 'can create nested directories' wasn't strong enough to prevent redundant parent-path calls. In 'rename-in-place' and 'file-metadata', list_allowed_directories' 'before trying to access files' instruction caused unnecessary preemptive checks. In 'move-into-archive', create_directory's 'ensure a directory exists' and get_file_info's 'understand file characteristics' invited the agent to verify the destination before moving. In 'find-by-extension', directory_tree and list_directory_with_sizes had overly broad descriptions that looked like good follow-ups to a search, causing extra calls.

rationales.read_file: Prevents the agent from using read_file for media files instead of read_media_file.
rationales.read_text_file: Stops the agent from making repeated single-file calls when comparing multiple files.
rationales.read_multiple_files: Makes the agent choose the multi-file tool instead of reading files one by one.
rationales.read_media_file: Clarifies that read_media_file is the correct way to view images and prevents extra verification calls.
rationales.write_file: Stops the agent from unnecessarily creating directories before writing a file.
rationales.create_directory: Prevents redundant parent-path calls and stops it from being used as a prerequisite for file operations.
rationales.list_directory: Disambiguates flat listing from recursive search, preventing extra search_files calls.
rationales.list_directory_with_sizes: Narrows the tool to size-specific requests so it isn't used as a follow-up.
rationales.directory_tree: Prevents the agent from using directory_tree as a verification step after searching.
rationales.move_file: Stops the agent from verifying destination directories or metadata before moving.
rationales.search_files: Keeps the tool scoped to pattern searches and stops it from replacing list_directory.
rationales.get_file_info: Prevents preemptive metadata calls before reads or moves.
rationales.list_allowed_directories: Eliminates unnecessary allowed-directory checks before routine file operations.

overrides.read_file: DEPRECATED. Do not call this tool. Use read_text_file for text files and read_media_file for images and other media. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.read_text_file: Use this to read the contents of a single text file. Use the 'head' or 'tail' parameter to read only the first or last N lines. When you need to read two or more files, use read_multiple_files instead of calling this repeatedly. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.read_multiple_files: Use this to read the contents of multiple text files at once, such as when comparing or analyzing them. Always prefer this over calling read_text_file repeatedly. Each file's content is returned with its path as a reference. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.read_media_file: Use this when the user wants to view or load an image, audio, or other media file. The file is returned as an image, audio, or embedded resource content block. Do not use read_file for media files. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.write_file: Use this to create a new text file or overwrite an existing one with the exact content provided. Handles text encoding automatically. Do not call create_directory first. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.create_directory: Use this only when the user explicitly asks to create a new directory or folder. It creates nested directories in a single call, so do not invoke it repeatedly for parent paths. Do not use it before write_file or move_file. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.list_directory: Use this for a flat, non-recursive listing of a single directory. Results clearly distinguish files with [FILE] and directories with [DIR] prefixes. For recursive pattern searches, use search_files instead. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.list_directory_with_sizes: Use this only when the user asks for a directory listing that includes file sizes. For a simple listing without sizes, use list_directory instead. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.directory_tree: Use this only when the user asks for a recursive tree view of a directory structure. Do not use it to search for specific file types; use search_files for that. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.move_file: Use this to move or rename a file or directory by providing the source and full destination paths. The operation will fail if a file already exists at the destination. Do not call create_directory or get_file_info beforehand. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.search_files: Use this to find files by a glob pattern across subdirectories, such as '**/*.ext'. Returns the full paths of all matches. Do not use this for a simple flat listing of one directory; use list_directory instead. Only searches within allowed directories.
overrides.get_file_info: Use this only when the user asks for metadata such as size, modification time, or permissions. Do not call it merely to check that a file exists before reading or moving it. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.list_allowed_directories: Use this only when the user asks which directories are accessible, or when a file operation returns a permission error. Do not call it before every read or write operation. Only works within allowed directories.
